topic: calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification

scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: The calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification is feasible on your MacBook M4 with 36GB RAM and MPS GPU, as these specifications can handle moderate-sized medical datasets and deep learning models efficiently. Suitable public datasets include the ChestX-ray14 dataset (112,120 chest X-rays), the ISIC Archive (20,000 skin lesion images), and the COVID-19 Dataset (10,000 chest X-rays). You can expect experiment completion within 8-12 hours, including data preprocessing, mode

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper introduces an automated pipeline that can perform the entire scientific discovery process, from idea generation to paper writing.
limitations: Limits in evaluation and reliability
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper highlights a significant gap in how to reliably evaluate the quality and novelty of the AI's generated scientific findings.

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
parsed_papers.core_claim: The AI Scientist-v2 extends automated scientific discovery by implementing stronger search and review-like iterations to enforce stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.
limitations: The system is evaluated on a 'workshop-level' standard, which may not generalize to more ambitious scientific discovery tasks.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The system's reliance on existing scientific literature for its 'search' phase may limit its ability to generate truly paradigm-shifting hypotheses that fall outside current knowledge.

parsed_papers.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
parsed_papers.core_claim: Agent Laboratory demonstrates a system for using LLM agents to automate and assist across the entire research lifecycle.
limitations: The approach requires significant human oversight and robust reproducibility controls.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: A key gap is the development of standardized frameworks for ensuring reproducibility and effective human-in-the-loop oversight when using autonomous agents for complex tasks.

parsed_papers.title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper introduces MLE-bench, a benchmark for evaluating machine learning agents on their ability to perform machine learning engineering tasks.
method_or_focus: Creation of a benchmark for ML engineering tasks.
Evaluation of agent performance on these tasks.
limitations: Limitations are not mentioned in the provided abstract.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not address how the benchmark tasks are curated to reflect the most current and challenging problems in real-world machine learning engineering.

parsed_papers.title: AIBuildAI-2: Scalable Agentic AI Research and Engineering
parsed_papers.core_claim: Frames AI systems that build and improve AI systems, including multi-agent research and engineering loops with traceability requirements.
limitations: Complexity of implementing multi-agent systems with end-to-end traceability
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Lack of frameworks for autonomous, multi-agent systems that can perform end-to-end research and engineering while maintaining full traceability.

parsed_papers.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
parsed_papers.core_claim: Reflexion uses verbal feedback and memory to help language agents improve their performance across multiple trials.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not specify the source or mechanism for generating the verbal feedback.

parsed_papers.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper studies iterative generation, feedback, and refinement to provide background for reviewer-style critique and revision policies.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper provides background on reviewer-style policies but does not evaluate their effectiveness.

parsed_papers.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
parsed_papers.core_claim: Voyager demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition in an embodied environment.
limitations: Not specified in the provided text
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not discuss the scalability of the approach or its performance in more complex, less structured environments.

parsed_papers.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
parsed_papers.core_claim: Structured benchmarks for agent behavior are necessary for proper evaluation, moving beyond isolated text quality.
limitations: Existing evaluation methods are limited by focusing on isolated text quality rather than holistic agent behavior.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: There is a lack of structured benchmarks that evaluate the holistic behavior of LLM agents, with current methods focusing too narrowly on text quality.

parsed_papers.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
parsed_papers.core_claim: The SWE-agent demonstrates that agents can operate computer interfaces to solve software engineering tasks.
limitations: The method is limited to tasks related to local experiment execution and reproducible command logs.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper focuses on local tasks, leaving a gap in applying this approach to large-scale, distributed software engineering environments.

parsed_papers.title: Bayesian Active Learning-Based Soft Data Space Calibration for System-Wise Aggregate Flexibility Characterization
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper proposes a network-aware, near-optimal, and sample-efficient data-driven method to accurately characterize the power flexibility region (PFR) for system-wide DER integration.
limitations: Existing methods for characterizing the power flexibility region (PFR) are computationally challenging due to DER heterogeneity and complex network constraints.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract is incomplete, so the full details of the proposed method, particularly the 'bus-level' component, are not available.

parsed_papers.title: AL-X0: Cost-Aware Active Learning for Cloud-Scale NLP via Zero-Shot Proxy Valuation
parsed_papers.core_claim: AL-X0 is a principled framework that addresses cold-start instability and cost collapse in cost-aware active learning by decoupling information valuation.
limitations: Cold-start instability where untrained models produce uncalibrated uncertainty estimates.
Cost collapse where naive cost-aware heuristics degrade model quality by selecting trivial samples.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The framework is designed for cloud-scale NLP, and its effectiveness on other data modalities or in non-cloud distributed settings remains an open question.

parsed_papers.title: RewardUQ: A Unified Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Reward Models
parsed_papers.core_claim: Reward models are central to aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences. Yet most approaches rely on pointwise reward estimates that overlook the epistemic uncertainty in reward mode
limitations: Uncertainty-aware reward models have been adopted without thorough comparison, leading to poor understanding of their effectiveness
unstated_weaknesses: Focuses on reward models for LLMs rather than image classification
Application domain is different (language models vs. medical images)
Unclear transferability of methods to medical imaging domain

parsed_papers.title: Bayesian E(3)-Equivariant Interatomic Potential with Iterative Restratification of Many-body Message Passing
parsed_papers.core_claim: Bayesian E(3)-equivariant MLPs with iterative restratification of many-body message passing using joint energy-force NLL loss achieve competitive accuracy while enabling uncertainty-guided applications.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Current machine learning potentials (MLPs) struggle with uncertainty quantification, limiting reliability for active learning, calibration, and out-of-distribution detection.
key_contributions: Development of joint energy-force negative log-likelihood (NLLJEF) loss function for explicit uncertainty modeling in both energies and forces
Systematic benchmarking of multiple Bayesian approaches (deep ensembles, SWAG, IVON, Laplace) across UQ tasks including calibration, OOD detection, and active learning
parsed_papers.novelty: Joint energy-force uncertainty modeling via NLLJEF fundamentally improves the accuracy-uncertainty trade-off compared to energy-only uncertainty approaches, enabling Bayesian MLPs to match deterministic model performance while providing reliable uncertainties.
parsed_papers.method_summary: The framework employs Bayesian E(3)-equivariant neural networks with iterative restratification of many-body message passing, trained using NLLJEF loss to simultaneously optimize energy/force predictions and their uncertainties, evaluated through unified validation metrics.
method_components: E(3)-equivariant message passing architecture with iterative restratification
Joint energy-force uncertainty modeling via NLLJEF loss
Multiple Bayesian inference methods (DE, SWAG, IVON, Laplace) for uncertainty quantification
parsed_papers.key_innovation: NLLJEF loss function that incorporates force uncertainties into the optimization objective, reducing energy errors by 3-4× and force errors by 2-3× compared to energy-only uncertainty approaches while maintaining competitive accuracy.
assumptions: Joint energy-force uncertainty modeling is superior to energy-only approaches
Uncertainty estimates are crucial for practical applications like active learning
Diverse Bayesian methods can be fairly compared through unified metrics
method_or_focus: NLLJEF loss function that incorporates force uncertainties into the optimization objective, reducing energy errors by 3-4× and force errors by 2-3× compared to energy-only uncertainty approaches while maintaining competitive accuracy.
limitations: The paper does not explicitly state limitations in the provided abstract
Likely computational complexity for large molecular systems
Potential scalability issues with Bayesian approaches
unstated_weaknesses: Domain specificity limits generalizability to other applications
Equivariant constraints may reduce model flexibility
Limited comparison with state-of-the-art methods in the abstract
Potential overfitting to specific molecular systems
threats_to_validity: Validation may be limited to specific molecular types
Potential bias toward systems similar to training data
Lack of diverse benchmark datasets mentioned
open_questions: How does the method scale to larger molecular systems?
What are the exact trade-offs between accuracy and computational efficiency?
How well does it perform on out-of-distribution molecular configurations?
future_work: Applying to more diverse molecular configurations
Improving computational efficiency of the Bayesian approach

Iterative restratification techniques for message passing

parsed_papers.title: A Risk-Tiered Validation Framework for Artificial Intelligence in Drug Discovery: From Reproducibility to Clinical Translation
parsed_papers.core_claim: New foundation models introduced between 2024 and 2026, such as BioEmu and AlphaFlow, are addressing fundamental concerns previously identified in AI applications for drug discovery.
limitations: The new models have only begun to address fundamental concerns, suggesting the validation process is ongoing.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract is incomplete, cutting off at 'Nevertheles[s]', which likely introduces a key limitation or gap in the research.

parsed_papers.title: CLASSIFICATION OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE TOOLING IN ARCHITECTURE. PROCESS-CYCLE DIMENSION
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper proposes a new classification system for AI tools in architecture based on the process-cycle axis.
method_or_focus: Parameter Alignment with RIBA Plan of Work
limitations: The classification is limited to the two specified parameters ('Process Stage', 'ML Lifecycle') and may not capture other relevant dimensions of AI tooling.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper introduces a classification but does not provide a framework for empirically validating the claimed 'highest value' of tools at different stages.

parsed_papers.title: Uncertainty-Guided Dual-Domain Learning for Reliable Skin Lesion Segmentation
parsed_papers.core_claim: We propose the Uncertainty-Guided Dual-Domain Network (UGDD-Net), which introduces a novel 'Glance-and-Gaze' mechanism to transform prediction uncertainty into an active guiding signal for more reliable skin lesion segmentation.
limitations: blind cross-domain fusion
overfitting to label noise
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper focuses on technical accuracy but does not address the clinical validation or impact of the improved segmentation on diagnostic outcomes.

parsed_papers.title: Active Learning for Pneumonia Detection with Vision Transformers and Bayesian Uncertainty Estimation
parsed_papers.core_claim: This study proposes a novel framework that integrates Vision Transformers (ViT) with Monte Carlo (MC) Dropout for uncertainty-aware pneumonia detection.
limitations: Reliance on large annotated datasets
Inability to quantify predictive uncertainty
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract does not specify how the 'Active Learning' component is integrated with the ViT and MC Dropout methods.

parsed_papers.title: AI Hydrologic Nowcasting in HEC-HMS: Graph Neural Nets under Nonstationarity
parsed_papers.core_claim: This study proposes an AI hydrologic nowcasting framework that couples HEC-HMS process states with graph neural networks (GNNs) to address nonstationarity.
limitations: Purely process-based or data-driven models are degraded by nonstationarity.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract is cut off, leaving the full description of the 'diffusion co...' method unknown.

parsed_papers.title: Probabilistic Embeddings for Frozen Vision-Language Models: Uncertainty Quantification with Gaussian Process Latent Variable Models
parsed_papers.core_claim: GroVE achieves state-of-the-art uncertainty calibration by obtaining probabilistic embeddings from frozen VLMs through a post-hoc Gaussian Process Latent Variable Model approach.
parsed_papers.problem_addressed: Deterministic embeddings from standard vision-language models struggle to capture uncertainties arising from ambiguities in visual/textual descriptions and multiple possible correspondences between modalities.
key_contributions: GroVE framework for post-hoc probabilistic embeddings from frozen VLMs
GPLVM-based shared latent space learning with single-modal reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives
Demonstrated superior uncertainty calibration across cross-modal retrieval, VQA, and active learning tasks
parsed_papers.novelty: Unlike prior probabilistic embedding methods requiring VLM retraining, GroVE operates post-hoc on frozen VLMs (e.g., CLIP, BLIP) using GPLVM to learn uncertainty-aware representations without modifying the original model.
parsed_papers.method_summary: GroVE builds a shared low-dimensional latent space using GPLVM where image and text inputs map to unified representations, optimized through single-modal embedding reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives to generate uncertainty-aware probabilistic embeddings.
method_components: Gaussian Process Latent Variable Model (GPLVM) framework
Single-modal embedding reconstruction objective
Cross-modal alignment objective
parsed_papers.key_innovation: Leveraging GPLVM's predictive distribution p(g*) = ∫ p(g*|x*)p(x*|z*, zM)dx* to capture uncertainty from both latent space uncertainty and inherent noise, enabling uncertainty quantification without VLM retraining.
assumptions: Frozen VLM embeddings provide meaningful representations for latent space learning
Low-dimensional latent space (Q=5 or 10) sufficiently captures cross-modal relationships
GP hyperparameters (inducing points, trade-off parameters) can be tuned via grid search
method_or_focus: Leveraging GPLVM's predictive distribution p(g*) = ∫ p(g*|x*)p(x*|z*, zM)dx* to capture uncertainty from both latent space uncertainty and inherent noise, enabling uncertainty quantification without VLM retraining.

parsed_papers.title: CTypiClust: Confidence-Aware Typical Clustering for Budget-Agnostic Active Learning with Confidence Calibration
parsed_papers.core_claim: This study proposes Conﬁdence-aware Typical Clustering (CTypiClust), an AL method that performs well regardless of the budget.
limitations: The abstract is incomplete, preventing a full understanding of the method's limitations.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Existing active learning methods are not effective across different annotation budgets, requiring a switch in strategy.

parsed_papers.title: Boundary Conditions of Cost-Aware Active Learning: A Multi-Dataset Taxonomy of Calibration and Length-Variance Failure Modes
parsed_papers.core_claim: This paper systematically studies the performance boundaries of the CAL-Log heuristic to understand its stability across diverse text classification tasks.
limitations: The stability of cost-aware selection is poorly understood across different types of text (linguistic regimes).
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The stability of cost-aware selection across diverse linguistic regimes is poorly understood.

parsed_papers.title: A Leakage-Aware Drug Discovery Workflow for PKM2 and MAPK1 Integrating Scaffold Validation, Molecular Docking and Structural Triage
parsed_papers.core_claim: We developed a leakage-aware prioritization workflow for two cancer-relevant targets, PKM2 and MAPK1.
limitations: Focuses on only two specific targets (PKM2 and MAPK1).
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The workflow's effectiveness needs to be validated on a wider range of targets and datasets beyond the LIT-PCBA benchmark to confirm its generalizability.

parsed_papers.title: Large language models in programming: a meta-analysis of tools, users, and human-computer interaction themes
parsed_papers.core_claim: This meta-analysis provides a comprehensive view of the opportunities and challenges posed by large language models in programming.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The study synthesizes existing research but does not present new primary data or in-depth analysis of a single tool.

parsed_papers.title: Uncertainty-aware phase fraction prediction and active-learning-guided out-of-domain discovery of refractory multi-principal element alloys
parsed_papers.core_claim: This study presents a deep learning framework using Mixture Density Networks to predict phase fractions in refractory multi-principal element alloys while accounting for prediction uncertainty.
limitations: Existing machine learning approaches are deterministic and neglect prediction uncertainty.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Future work could explore using the model's uncertainty estimates to actively guide the selection of new compositions for experimental validation.

parsed_papers.title: Small Business Innovation Research Program
parsed_papers.core_claim: The Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program is a critical driver of technological innovation and economic growth in the United States.
method_or_focus: Quantitative analysis of SBIR funding data and patent outputs
Qualitative case studies of SBIR-funded recipients
limitations: Difficulty in establishing direct causality between SBIR funding and long-term business success.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: A lack of research on the long-term impact of SBIR funding on the sustainability of small businesses beyond the initial grant period.

parsed_papers.title: Revisiting Unknowns: Towards Effective and Efficient Open-Set Active Learning
parsed_papers.core_claim: We propose E$^2$OAL, a unified and detector-free framework that effectively and efficiently uses labeled unknowns to improve both known-class and unknown-class learning.
limitations: Existing approaches have substantial training overhead and overlook the value of labeled unknowns for learning.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The abstract doesn't specify the quantitative performance gains or the computational efficiency metrics compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.

parsed_papers.title: OmniDP: Beyond-FOV Large-Workspace Humanoid Manipulation with Omnidirectional 3D Perception
parsed_papers.core_claim: OmniDP enables humanoid robots to perform dexterous manipulation in a large workspace without requiring base movement by leveraging omnidirectional 3D perception.
limitations: Narrow fields of view (FOV)
Self-occlusion
Requirement for frequent base movements
parsed_papers.possible_gap: There is a lack of methods that allow humanoid robots to maintain omnidirectional awareness for manipulation in large workspaces without needing to physically reposition themselves.

parsed_papers.title: Physics-guided big-data reflectance spectroscopy learning framework for industrial surface state recognition
parsed_papers.core_claim: We propose a physics-guided big-data learning framework to automatically recognize the surface state of industrial materials using reflectance spectroscopy, overcoming the limitations of traditional methods.
limitations: Existing methods rely on hand-crafted features and small datasets, making them brittle to real-world variations like material changes and sensor drift.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The proposed framework is a conceptual proposal; its practical effectiveness and performance on real-world industrial data have not yet been demonstrated.

parsed_papers.title: Weapon Detection Using Deep Learning: A Systematic Review
parsed_papers.core_claim: We find that YOLO-family one-stage detectors dominate visible-spectrum applications by offering the best accuracy–latency trade-off for real-time inference.
limitations: The need for real-time inference creates a trade-off between model accuracy and latency, which is a key deployment constraint.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The review focuses on visible-spectrum and X-ray modalities, potentially overlooking advancements in other sensing technologies like thermal or infrared for weapon detection.

parsed_papers.title: MOSE: A Monotonic Selectivity Estimator Using Learned CDF
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper proposes MOSE, a learning-based selectivity estimator that uses a learned Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF).
limitations: high estimation latency
large training data demanding
occurrence of illogical results
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper aims to address the limitations of existing learning-based methods, such as high latency and illogical results, by proposing a more interpretable and efficient approach.

method_or_focus: Local evaluation of Dupire's equation using neural network differentiability

idea_tree.title: Automated Calibration-Aware Active Learning Pipeline with RewardUQ
idea_tree.pitch: A fully automated pipeline that integrates calibration-aware active learning with uncertainty quantification for medical image classification.
idea_tree.mechanism: RewardUQ framework automatically selects informative samples while maintaining model calibration through uncertainty-aware sampling.

idea_tree.title: Calibration-Aware Active Learning Benchmark Suite
idea_tree.pitch: A comprehensive benchmark suite specifically designed to evaluate calibration-aware active learning methods in medical imaging.
idea_tree.mechanism: Standardized evaluation metrics and protocols for assessing both classification performance and calibration quality across multiple medical imaging tasks.
idea_tree.metric: Composite score of accuracy and ECE

idea_tree.title: Calibration-Aware LLM Agent with RewardUQ Integration
idea_tree.pitch: Specialized LLM agents that use RewardUQ framework to make calibration-aware decisions about medical image labeling.
idea_tree.mechanism: LLM agents analyze RewardUQ uncertainty estimates to prioritize samples that improve both performance and calibration.

idea_tree.title: Information-Theoretic Iterative Refinement for Calibration
idea_tree.pitch: An iterative refinement approach that uses information theory to progressively improve model calibration.
idea_tree.mechanism: Information-theoretic measures guide iterative model updates to minimize calibration error while maintaining accuracy.

idea_tree.title: Probabilistic Embeddings for Calibration-Aware Sampling
idea_tree.pitch: Using probabilistic embeddings to create a calibration-aware benchmark for medical image classification.
idea_tree.mechanism: GroVE with KL-Divergence generates embeddings that capture both feature similarity and calibration uncertainty.

idea_tree.title: Anomaly Detection Guided Calibration-Aware Active Learning
idea_tree.pitch: Using anomaly detection to identify and prioritize samples that most improve model calibration.
idea_tree.mechanism: Anomaly detection identifies out-of-distribution samples that, when labeled, significantly improve calibration.

method_matrix.title: RewardUQ: A Unified Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Reward Models
method_matrix.method: Reward models are central to aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences. Yet most approaches rely on pointwise reward estimates that overlook the epistemic uncertainty in reward mode
limitations: Uncertainty-aware reward models have been adopted without thorough comparison, leading to poor understanding of their effectiveness

method_matrix.title: Bayesian E(3)-Equivariant Interatomic Potential with Iterative Restratification of Many-body Message Passing
method_matrix.method: The framework employs Bayesian E(3)-equivariant neural networks with iterative restratification of many-body message passing, trained using NLLJEF loss to simultaneously optimize energy/force predictions and their uncertainties, evaluated through unified validation metrics.
limitations: The paper does not explicitly state limitations in the provided abstract
Likely computational complexity for large molecular systems
Potential scalability issues with Bayesian approaches

method_matrix.title: Probabilistic Embeddings for Frozen Vision-Language Models: Uncertainty Quantification with Gaussian Process Latent Variable Models
method_matrix.method: GroVE builds a shared low-dimensional latent space using GPLVM where image and text inputs map to unified representations, optimized through single-modal embedding reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives to generate uncertainty-aware probabilistic embeddings.
method_matrix.best_result: GroVE with KL-Divergence achieves best cross-modal alignment

method_matrix.title: Information-theoretic Generalization Analysis for Expected Calibration Error
method_matrix.method: Establishes upper bounds on ECE estimation bias for uniform mass and uniform width binning, derives optimal bin sizes, and extends analysis to generalization error using information-theoretic bounds to evaluate ECE on unknown data.

method_matrix.title: Unified Anomaly Detection methods on Edge Device using Knowledge Distillation and Quantization
method_matrix.method: The paper implements three knowledge distillation frameworks (US, RD, STFPM) to train unified multi-class models, then applies quantization techniques (PTQ and QAT) for edge deployment, analyzing performance with different precision widths.
method_matrix.best_result: STFPM achieves 52.14ms latency with 0.94 AUROC on Jetson (FP-32)
method_matrix.compute_required: CPU and NVIDIA Jetson NX device

method_matrix.title: Improving Robustness and Reliability in Medical Image Classification With Latent-Guided Diffusion and Nested-Ensembles
method_matrix.method: Once deployed, medical image analysis methods are often faced with unexpected image corruptions and noise perturbations. These unknown covariate shifts present significant challenges to deep learning
method_matrix.best_result: 94.86% accuracy on Chest X-ray against FGSM attack
limitations: The paper focuses on addressing robustness and reliability in medical image classification but doesn't explicitly state limitations.
The abstract doesn't mention specific limitations of the LaDiNE approach.
